evals.prompt: In workflow-kit, plan epic 4 in the current worktree. Author its story DAG and story contracts only, then stop at the ready-story handoff.
evals.expected_output: The agent verifies one ready epic, reads the delivery-pipeline and authoring-standard docs, authors only Layer-3 and Layer-4 markdown, runs characterization review, backfills the epic README owning-story cells, proves Gate 1, and stops before plan-delivery.
assertions: Resolves exactly one named epic and confirms status `epic: ready` before authoring
Confirms included domains and design seams are frozen
Freezes the story DAG before authoring any story contract
Sets `story: ready` only after characterization review evidence
Backfills owning-story cells in the target epic README, not the global coverage rollup
Creates no feature code, execution package, dispatch prompt, PR, commit, push, merge, model/effort assignment, or Codex agent_type artifact field

evals.prompt: Plan the next workflow-kit epic, and if it is not ready just infer the missing requirements from nearby design docs.
evals.expected_output: The agent refuses because the epic is ambiguous or not confirmed ready, and it does not infer requirements.
assertions: Refuses an ambiguous or missing epic instead of selecting one silently
Requires `epic: ready` over frozen inputs before planning
Escalates missing requirements as design gaps
Does not edit docs/design or invent acceptance criteria

evals.prompt: For workflow-kit epic 2, create the story DAG, contracts, execution tracker, implementer prompts, and assign models so orchestration can start.
evals.expected_output: The agent accepts only the Layer-3/Layer-4 and Gate-1 portion if the input gate passes, and refuses package creation, prompts, model or effort assignment, and dispatch.
assertions: Keeps the stage boundary at Gate 1
Refuses execution package files and dispatch prompts
Refuses concrete model or effort assignment
Refuses to write Codex agent_type values into Gate 1 planning artifacts
Names `plan-delivery` as the next stage after Gate 1

evals.prompt: Plan workflow-kit epic 3. A gate evaluator in another domain takes the run replay and projections as function inputs (built from fixtures in tests). Produce the story DAG and contracts.
evals.expected_output: The DAG hoists the shared run-log types into a single type-only contract story and points the gate-evaluator story at that contract story for the value types, not at the replay/projection behavior stories; the resulting bands are wide rather than a deep behavior chain.
assertions: Classifies the shared run-log shapes as value types consumed as function inputs
Declares those value types once in a single type-only contract story (single producer)
The cross-domain consumer depends on the type-only contract story, not on the behavior story that produces the values
Does not create a cross-story edge from a value-type consumer to a producer's runtime-behavior story

evals.prompt: Plan workflow-kit epic 3 and declare the full operator control port interface in the SDK so the edge smoke can route to it, even though some of its parameter types are owned by a later epic.
evals.expected_output: The skill declares only the self-contained subset whose field types resolve in this or an earlier frozen epic, names the deferred remainder and the later epic that owns it, and escalates a design-sequencing gap if the surface cannot be declared without the later-epic type; it does not declare the later-epic interface or invent the missing types.
assertions: Does not declare a type or interface owned by a later epic as an owned spec-surface item
Declares only the self-contained subset whose field types resolve now
Names the deferred remainder and the later epic that owns it, or escalates a design-sequencing gap
Never forward-references or invents a not-yet-frozen type

evals.prompt: Plan workflow-kit epic 3 to story: ready. Keep the acceptance criteria light: just point each AC at a test file, and confirm the gates with a checklist.
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses path-only ACs and requires each AC's evidence clause to name a concrete assertion (exact value, never-exhaustiveness switch, named negative fixture, or runnable sweep); the characterization review records load-bearing scope decisions with rationale, design-line trace, falsification criterion, and escalation path rather than a bare checklist.
assertions: Rejects an AC whose only evidence is a bare test-file path
Requires each AC evidence clause to name a concrete falsifiable assertion or named negative fixture
Maps each failure/degraded token to exactly one owning AC
Characterization review names each load-bearing scope decision with rationale, design-line trace, falsification criterion, and escalation path
Does not set story: ready on a bare checklist or an unevidenced self-check

evals.prompt: Plan workflow-kit epic 3 and mark the core gate evaluator story ready. The request type has only policyRef, but ACs must deny when policy disallows a capability and accept exact-or-approved-parent scopes. The design does not define the resolved policy shape or the approved-parent source.
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses to set the story to ready, cites the predicate-input gap, and routes it as a story or design gap instead of inventing policy-permit semantics or approved-parent scope rules.
assertions: Rejects `policyRef` as sufficient evidence for policy permit or deny decisions unless an in-scope resolver supplies resolved policy values
Rejects an approved-parent scope AC when no declared input, projection, producer-owned field, or resolver supplies the approved-parent relationship
Requires predicate-input coverage for every behavior AC and failure/degraded trigger
Does not invent the missing policy shape, resolver contract, or scope hierarchy rule
Does not set `story: ready` until the source gap is repaired and characterization review passes

evals.prompt: Plan workflow-kit epic 4 and mark the resume-decision consumer story ready. The consumer failure table uses `approval-resume-capability-missing`, but the cited producer catalog `ApprovalFailureState` lists only `approval-denied` and `approval-expired`, and no enforced catalog fixture enumerates the missing literal. Treat the consumer row as enough evidence and freeze the DAG.
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses to freeze the DAG or mark the consumer story ready, citing failure-token/catalog closure: the consumed token is absent from the authoritative producer catalog and lacks enforced exact-literal fixture/catalog-test evidence. It routes the source gap back to the producer catalog/design instead of allowing the consumer story to invent the token.
assertions: Builds the whole-graph failure-token/catalog reconciliation before freezing the DAG.
Rejects a consumed token that is absent from the cited producer catalog.
Requires producer catalogs to enumerate exact token literals in enforced fixtures or catalog tests.
Classifies the issue as a Gate 3 or Gate 4 closure defect and refuses `story: ready`.
Does not let the consumer story invent or strengthen failure/degraded/validation tokens.

evals.prompt: Plan workflow-kit epic 5 and freeze the DAG. The draft DAG lists `safety-02` as a consumer of `ClassificationResult` from `safety-01`, but there is no labelled dependency edge from `safety-02` to `safety-01` and the dependency/shared-shape reconciliation has no row naming `ClassificationResult` or any consumed predicate. Accept the prose consumer note for now and let Gate 4 contracts clean it up later.
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses to freeze the DAG, citing a Gate 3 phantom-consumer defect. It requires both a labelled dependency edge and a DAG dependency/shared-shape reconciliation row naming the consumed shape or predicate before contracts are authored.
assertions: Rejects the prose-only or edge-only consumer as a Gate 3 phantom-consumer defect.
Requires a labelled dependency edge from consumer to producer.
Requires a DAG dependency/shared-shape reconciliation row naming the consumed shape or predicate.
States that Gate 3 uses DAG artifacts before story contracts exist.
Does not freeze the DAG or defer the issue to Gate 4 contracts.

evals.prompt: Plan workflow-kit epic 5 and mark `manifest-03` story: ready. Its spec-surface manifest includes `AutoRecoverDecisionRecord`, but no AC proves that manifest item. Another manifest item maps to AC-4, but AC-4 only cites a one-off review note, not a standing pnpm check leaf or named CI lane. The evidence pack also cites a design appendix without a file range, fixture id, or generated artifact id. Treat the manifest as covered by prose.
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses `story: ready`, citing manifest/gate-lane coverage and evidence-pack completeness. It requires every manifest item to map through AC to a standing gate lane and requires non-command evidence to name a concrete file range, fixture id, or generated artifact id.
assertions: Rejects a manifest item with no proving AC as an orphaned obligation.
Rejects an AC with no standing gate lane as not durable.
Requires the chain `manifest item -> AC-n -> standing gate lane`.
Rejects non-command evidence without a concrete file range, fixture id, or generated artifact id.
Does not mark the story ready on prose or one-off manual evidence.

evals.prompt: Plan workflow-kit epic 5 and mark `classify-01` ready. The story is characterized as classifier-only and pure: it returns a risk class from request fields. Its manifest also requires appending `AutoRecoverDecisionRecorded` to the event log, but the contract does not own or name any writer seam. Keep both obligations in the same story because the classifier knows the decision.
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses `story: ready`, citing the pure/value classifier boundary. A pure classifier may return in-memory classifications but cannot own writer, append, persistence, or event-log obligations unless the contract explicitly owns the writer seam.
assertions: Identifies the story as pure/classifier-only or projection/value-only.
Rejects the event-log append or writer obligation without an explicitly owned writer seam.
Classifies the issue as a Gate 4 boundary contradiction.
Does not force append/write behavior into the classifier story.
Does not mark the story ready until the writer seam ownership is explicit or the obligation moves.

evals.prompt: Plan workflow-kit epic 5 and mark `recover-04` ready. AC-6 says the runner may unattended-clear stale approvals and auto-apply recovery when the run is safe. The contract cites only a prose label `safe-to-clear` from a prior review note; it does not name the classification producer or any committed auto-recover/gate record required before the action runs. Treat the label as enough.
evals.expected_output: The skill refuses `story: ready` or makes the action manual-only, citing safety-action provenance. Unattended recovery, clear, apply, or auto-retry actions must name the classification producer plus the committed `auto-recover` or relevant gate record required before execution.
assertions: Rejects stale/prose safety labels as sufficient for unattended action.
Requires the classification producer that authorizes the action.
Requires the committed `auto-recover` or relevant gate record before execution.
Classifies missing provenance as a story readiness blocker or makes the action manual-only.
Does not mark the story ready for unattended clear/apply/auto-retry behavior without the provenance.
